title: apply.py mypy debt (un-suppressed): get_parse_function's Callable type omits school/grounding kwargs -> 2 call-arg + 1 unused-ignore; gate RED
description: The B1 negligence-suppression (pyproject [[tool.mypy.overrides]] module=['dispatch.apply'] ignore_errors=true) has been REMOVED, re-exposing apply.py's real mypy errors to the standing `mypy .`: (1) apply.py:294 `parse_fn(response_text, school=school, grounding=grounding)` produces 2 `call-arg` errors (Unexpected keyword argument 'school' / 'grounding') because `parse_fn = get_parse_function(args.spec_key)` (prompt-workshop/dispatch) returns a Callable whose type does NOT declare the school/grounding keyword params -- although every concrete parser accepts `*, school=None, grounding=None` and it works at runtime (a typing imprecision, not a runtime bug); (2) apply.py:268 carries an unused `# type: ignore`. With the override gone, `mypy .` is RED (3 errors / 314 files), so the standing gate + the PreToolUse commit hook BLOCK all commits until apply.py is fixed. This is the apply.py slice of FGAP-010 (whole-dispatch-dir gate), now un-suppressed and ACTIVE (a blocker, not deferred debt). apply.py + apply_from_substrate.py are ruff-clean.

evidence.reference: return Callable type omits the school/grounding kwargs the concrete parsers accept

impact: mypy . RED -> the standing gate + the commit hook block ALL commits (main + subagent) until resolved. Surfaced by undoing a negligent B1 suppression of known, trivially-fixable typing debt.
proposed_resolution: Widen get_parse_function's return type (e.g. a Protocol or precise Callable declaring __call__(self, text: str, *, school=..., grounding=...)) so apply.py:294 type-checks; remove the unused # type: ignore at apply.py:268. Then mypy . green; apply.py genuinely gated. Add ruff check of apply.py/apply_from_substrate.py to the Makefile/CI gate. Run via the canonical pipeline.
